relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Ancient enslaved hearers are addressed inside a constrained social order.
Shared trust is called to reshape service without contempt.
Healthy words of Lord Jesus are the measure of teaching.
Puffed-up dispute culture understands nothing and breeds harm.
Corrupted minds turn reverence into profit.
Reverence with contentment is great gain.
Nothing is brought in; nothing is carried out.
Enoughness learns contentment with food and covering.
The craving to be rich becomes a snare of harmful desires.
Love of money leads many pains and can pull trust off course.
The human of Divine pursues justice, reverence, trust, love, endurance, and gentleness.
The beautiful struggle of trust takes hold of age-long life.
The charge is given before the Life-giving Divine and Messiah Jesus.
The command is guarded until the Lord’s appearing.
The blessed Sovereign reveals in the proper seasons.
The unseen One dwells in unapproachable light and receives honor.
The rich are warned not to hope in uncertain riches but in the generous Divine.
They are called to good works, generosity, and sharing.
True life is taken hold of through a better foundation.
Timothy guards the deposit and turns from empty noise.
False knowledge can miss the mark of trust; grace remains.

contemplative_note: 1 Timothy 6 closes with contentment and true life: not greed, not domination, not empty knowledge, but justice, gentleness, generosity, and the deposit of trust guarded in grace.
